title: SSH Local/Remote/Dynamic Port Forwarding
description: This runbook operationalizes SSH Local/Remote/Dynamic Port Forwarding using #ssh . It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments. Builds forward and reverse tunnels plus SOCKS transport for deeper network access.

metadata.descriptionHtml:
This runbook operationalizes SSH Local/Remote/Dynamic Port Forwarding using #ssh. It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments.

Builds forward and reverse tunnels plus SOCKS transport for deeper network access.

  1. Prepare scope and access: validate target reachability and select low-noise execution order.
  2. Execute core workflow: run the command chain to produce actionable security outcomes.
  3. Validate and operationalize output: confirm results, capture artifacts, and prepare follow-on actions.
  • Tool Location/Source: primary references are in local source corpus entries linked from runbook-list.md; e.g., docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/0x1ceKing-HTB-Certified-Penetration-Testing-Specialist/cheatsheet/Pivoting Tunneling Port Forwarding.md:7; docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/0x1ceKing-HTB-Certified-Penetration-Testing-Specialist/cheatsheet/Pivoting Tunneling Port Forwarding.md:23.
  • Attack pre-requisite: authorized scope, operator shell access, and protocol reachability on TCP 80, TCP 443.
  • What attacker gain: validated findings (access paths, credentials, execution context, or exposure evidence) enabling faster compromise progression.
  • Detection/Defense opsec: monitor abnormal auth/enumeration bursts, enforce least privilege and hardening baselines, and alert on repeated tool-pattern telemetry.

Core Commands:

ssh -L 1234:localhost:3306 <USER>@<PIVOT_IP>
ssh -R <PIVOT_IP>:8080:0.0.0.0:80 <USER>@<TARGET_IP> -vN
ssh -D 9050 <USER>@<PIVOT_IP>
prerequisites: authorized scope, operator shell access, and protocol reachability on TCP 80 , TCP 443 .
attackerGains: validated findings (access paths, credentials, execution context, or exposure evidence) enabling faster compromise progression.
detectionNotes: monitor abnormal auth/enumeration bursts, enforce least privilege and hardening baselines, and alert on repeated tool-pattern telemetry.
extraInfo: primary references are in local source corpus entries linked from runbook-list.md ; e.g., docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/0x1ceKing-HTB-Certified-Penetration-Testing-Specialist/cheatsheet/Pivoting Tunneling Port Forwarding.md:7; docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/0x1ceKing-HTB-Certified-Penetration-Testing-Specialist/cheatsheet/Pivoting Tunneling Port Forwarding.md:23 .
